SEO.manualTitle: Grid Assistant & Avatar Config: Complete User Manual
SEO.pixelGuideTitle: 1. Retro Pixel Studio — Multi-Ratio & Multi-Density Grid Converter (Pixel Studio Guide)
SEO.pixelStep1: Step 1 — Upload: Click or drag-and-drop any JPEG/PNG image (anime avatars, badges, icons, etc.) into the <pixel>upload area</pixel>.
SEO.pixelStep2: Step 2 — Canvas Ratio: Pick a preset ratio — Square (1:1) for icons, Book Cover (2:3) for portraits, or Interior Wide (16:9) for panoramas. The image auto-crops and centers to fit.
SEO.pixelStep3: Step 3 — Grid Density: Choose from four levels — 256×256 (1px, ultra-fine), 85×85 (3px, standard), 64×64 (4px, simplified), or 32×32 (7px, minimalist). Switching re-samples the canvas with anti-aliasing off for razor-sharp pixels.
SEO.pixelStep4: Step 4 — Color Tweaks: Brightness — if the original is too dark, bump it up to keep edge detail. Contrast — the secret sauce of pixel art! Crank it up a bit to sharpen lines and color boundaries so the grid doesn't look muddy.
SEO.pixelStep5: Step 5 — Universal 8-Bit <pixel>Retro Palette</pixel>: Flip 'Retro Match' to activate the built-in 32-color retro palette. Nearest-neighbor matching maps each pixel to the closest retro color (Retro Red, Classic Blue, Neon Yellow, etc.), cutting the color count way down for that classic pixel-art look. This is a generic palette — no copyrighted color data.
SEO.pixelStep6: Step 6 — <pixel>Paint-by-Numbers</pixel>: The color engine auto-quantizes and shows the top 24 colors as numbered swatches. Click any swatch to highlight matching pixels with dashed borders and index numbers. In Retro Mode, matched color names (e.g., 'Vintage Teal') appear below the palette — handy for tracing on your handheld.
SEO.pixelStep7: Step 7 — Tracing: The canvas uses image-rendering: pixelated, so it stays razor-sharp zoomed in. Follow the grid on-screen while painting in your handheld's palette system — save up to 80% color alignment time.
SEO.qrGuideTitle: 2. Avatar QR Code Auto-Detect Unlocker — Single Edit & Batch ZIP Export (QR Unlocker Guide)
SEO.qrStep1: Step 1 — Load <qr>QR Code(s)</qr>: Drag & drop or click to upload one or more QR code images. Auto-detect kicks in: 1 file = detailed edit mode; 2+ files = batch mode. Works with phone screenshots, web images, and full roster uploads.
SEO.qrStep2: Step 2 — Local Binary Parsing: jsQR reads the code(s) client-side, then parses the underlying avatar binary data (Buffer). In batch mode, all files parse in parallel via Promise.all — 20 files in under 0.5 seconds.
SEO.qrStep3: Step 3 — <qr>One-Click Unlock</qr>: Check 'Force Allow Copying' to override the anti-copy flag so you can save others' avatars to your roster. Check 'Force Allow Editing' to override the edit lock so you can tweak outfits, hairstyles, and settings after the avatar moves in. Both options work in single and batch modes.
SEO.qrStep4: Step 4 — Rename (single file): In single-file mode, type a new name and the system overwrites the matching binary byte segment. (Rename is single-file only for precision.)
SEO.qrStep5: Step 5 — Generate & <qr>Export ZIP</qr>: Single file — click 'Generate & Unlock' for a Version 3, M-level error correction QR code (Byte Mode), guaranteed to scan 100%. Batch — click 'Batch Unlock All & Export ZIP' to process everything in parallel and package it as unlocked-avatars.zip. One click, done!
SEO.voiceGuideTitle: 3. 8-Bit Voice Synth, Personality Matrix & Relationship Leaderboard (Voice Lab Guide)
SEO.voiceStep1: <voice>Voice Presets</voice>: Pick from 5 built-in voices — Adult Male (sawtooth 180Hz), Adult Female (sawtooth 350Hz), Elder (sawtooth 120Hz + vibrato LFO), Child (sawtooth 600Hz+ fast), and Robot (square wave 250Hz, hard envelope). Each preset dials in a unique waveform, filter, and envelope combo to simulate a different character.
SEO.voiceStep2: Voice Tuning: Adjust Pitch (100–800Hz) and Speed (0.5–2.0x). Hit 'Play Voice Test' to fire up a Web Audio API oscillator with the selected preset — tweak until you nail that perfect 8-bit robot voice.
SEO.voiceStep3: <voice>Custom Phrase TTS</voice>: Type any text (up to 100 chars) and click 'Speak Phrase'. Each character plays as a short beep with slight frequency variation — classic retro 'beep-beep-beep' robot speech. Great for making fun clips to share!
SEO.voiceStep4: <voice>Personality Matrix</voice>: Pick any of the 16 personality types and the system draws a 2×2 quadrant chart — Outgoing (amber), Confident (red), Independent (purple), Easygoing (green). A pulsing dot marks where the selected personality lands on the spectrum.
SEO.voiceStep5: Compatibility Calculator: In the 'Resident A' and 'Resident B' cards, pick their zodiac signs and personality types. Click 'Calculate Compatibility' for instant Romance and Friendship scores:
SEO.voiceLove: ❤️ Romance: Based on zodiac pairing (50%) + personality complement weighting (50%). More complementary = more sparks!
SEO.voiceFriend: 🤝 Friendship: Based on personality similarity + zodiac harmony. Same-group pairs tend to become inseparable besties.
SEO.voiceStep6: <voice>Relationship Leaderboard</voice>: Click 'Add Resident' to build an island roster of up to 15 (saved in localStorage — zero server cost). Enter each resident's name, zodiac, and personality. Hit 'Calculate All Pairings' for a full C(n,2) cross-comparison. Instantly see: 🏆 Top 3 Soulmates (highest romance) and 💥 Top 3 Rivals (lowest romance). Perfect for sharing on Reddit or TikTok!
SEO.q1: Will my uploaded photos, avatars, or QR codes be stored on your servers?
SEO.a1: Absolutely not — that's our core philosophy. LifeSimGrid runs 100% client-side. All pixelation, decoding, and QR generation happen right in your browser. We have no backend database and zero ability to receive your files. Your data stays on your device, period.
SEO.q2: Is this an official tool? Will using it get my console or account banned?
SEO.a2: No — this is a fan-made third-party tool, not affiliated with or endorsed by any console maker or game developer. It doesn't exploit console vulnerabilities or contact any online servers. It just processes standard image and binary data formats (Canvas, public avatar protocol) in the browser. Everything stays local — 100% safe, zero risk to your console or account.
SEO.q3: Why does the site show ads? Will it ever charge?
SEO.a3: This toolkit is and always will be free — no downloads, no sign-ups. But as the community grows on Reddit and TikTok, global CDN bandwidth costs add up. To keep the project sustainable, we may show a small number of non-tracking, high-quality tech/design ads. Every cent goes toward bandwidth and domain costs — no profit motive. Thanks for understanding!
SEO.q4: Why do complex photos look like a mess at 85×85?
SEO.a4: 85×85 is fairly dense, but still too small for high-res photos with lots of detail and gradients — forced compression just mangles them. Best bet: upload images with clean lines, solid colors, and strong contrast (anime avatars, logos, minimalist art). And crank up the Contrast slider — it sharpens blurry edges and makes the pixel output way better!
SEO.q5: Can this converter generate an image I can import directly into the game?
SEO.a5: Nope. The game's palette system doesn't support importing external image files (e.g., via SD card) — you have to paint manually in-game. Think of <pixel>Pixel Studio</pixel> as a tracing aid: instead of guessing grids and colors, just follow the on-screen grid to paint quickly and accurately on your handheld.
SEO.q13: Does the 8-Bit Retro Palette use official game colors?
SEO.a13: No. The 32-color palette is a generic, independently curated set with names like Retro Red, Classic Blue, Neon Yellow, and Vintage Teal. It doesn't copy any game's proprietary palette or grouping system. Nearest-neighbor matching (Euclidean distance) is standard math — purely a visual simplification tool, not a derivative of any copyrighted UI or color scheme.
SEO.q6: Why does uploading an avatar QR code give me 'Parse Failed' or 'Invalid Binary Data'?
SEO.a6: That usually means the QR code doesn't contain raw binary avatar data. Many QR codes shared online just hold a URL or plain text. Our parser only reads special QR codes with handheld console avatar binary standard binary streams. How to tell: real avatar QR codes are very dense and complex. Official islander QR codes scan perfectly every time. In batch mode, invalid files are auto-skipped and counted — valid ones process normally.
SEO.q7: Why does my console camera sometimes fail to read the unlocked QR code?
SEO.a7: This isn't an encoding issue — we use Byte Mode, so the binary stream is 100% intact. It's usually physical: turn your phone screen brightness to max, zoom in so the QR code fills the screen center, clean your console's camera lens, and scan in good lighting (avoid glare). That should do it.
SEO.q10: How does batch unlock and ZIP export work? Is there a file limit?
SEO.a10: Drop 2+ QR codes into the <qr>upload area</qr> and batch mode kicks in automatically. Files decode in parallel via Promise.all — 20 files in under 0.5s. Click 'Batch Unlock All & Export ZIP' to get a single unlocked-avatars.zip with each file named after the avatar (e.g., Alice-unlocked.png). No hard limit, but we recommend under 50 per batch for best performance.
SEO.q8: Does the voice synth sound exactly like the game?
SEO.a8: It's a close analog — <voice>Voice Lab</voice> uses the browser's Web Audio API for pure code synthesis. The original game uses proprietary hardware chips. Our sawtooth + lowpass filter chain nails that distinctive 8-bit 'electronic robot voice' feel, making it perfect for previewing pitch and speed before entering values on your handheld.
SEO.q9: If compatibility shows 100%, will they definitely get married or become best friends?
SEO.a9: The calculator uses a 12×12 zodiac chart + the game's 16-personality complement/similarity matrix for destiny compatibility — a great pairing guide. But in-game, relationships also depend on daily interactions, gift-giving, and random events. We calculate 'innate chemistry' — the rest is up to you!
SEO.q11: How does the Personality Matrix work?
SEO.a11: The 16 personality types fall into 4 groups: Outgoing, Confident, Independent, and Easygoing. The <voice>matrix</voice> plots them as a 2×2 quadrant chart with a pulsing dot for the selected type. Complementary pairs (Outgoing + Independent) tend to score higher in romance; same-group pairs (both Easygoing) tend to score higher in friendship.
SEO.q12: Will my resident roster data be uploaded to a server?
SEO.a12: Never. The roster lives entirely in your browser's localStorage — it never leaves your device. Zero server storage. Clear your browser data and it resets. By design: 100% client-side, zero privacy risk, zero server cost. Your island's secrets stay on your island!
SEO.q14: How do the five voice presets work? Are they game sound effects?
SEO.a14: They're 100% real-time synthesized using the Web Audio API — no game audio is sampled or reproduced. Each preset combines a unique waveform (sawtooth/square), base frequency, filter cutoff, and envelope. The Elder adds Vibrato LFO (5Hz) for a trembling effect; the Robot uses a square wave with zero attack/release for that hard mechanical sound. Pure digital synthesis.
SEO.q15: How does Custom Phrase TTS work? Can it speak any language?
SEO.a15: It converts each character into a short oscillator beep using the selected voice preset, with slight frequency variation per character — classic retro 'beep-beep-beep' robot speech. Spaces add longer pauses; every 5 characters gets an extra 'breath' pause. Supports any text (English, Japanese, Chinese, etc.) up to 100 chars. It's not traditional TTS — it's a creative 8-bit speech simulator for fun and social sharing.
SEO.q16: Why does Pixel Studio support so many canvas ratios and brush densities beyond 85×85?
SEO.a16: Because this site is positioned as a customization tool compatible with life simulation games. In today's mainstream social simulation games, players' creations are no longer limited to a single tile or outfit. When you need to design book covers, music albums, full-coverage furniture interiors, or even widescreen wall wallpapers, the game uses different crop ratios. This toolkit provides multi-spec canvases and multi-density brush sampling to perfectly adapt to the extreme creative needs across multiple social simulation games, freeing your pixel tracing from limitations.
SEO.q17: What is the main purpose of this site? What types of players is it for?
SEO.a17: This site is an auxiliary tool designed for island life simulation games. If you love building apartments on virtual islands, managing resident relationships, and hand-drawing pixel-style personalized outfits, then this pure frontend toolkit is your ultimate assistant. Through serverless Canvas image processing, binary data permission repair, and 8-bit handheld voice simulation technology, we comprehensively optimize your islander setup and drawing workflow — so you can focus more on pure creativity and fun while enjoying island life simulation games.
SEO.privacyBadge: 🔒 100% Client-Side Privacy — Everything runs in your browser. Zero data uploads.
SEO.disclaimer: Disclaimer: This site is an independent third-party client-side toolkit built with standard web tech. It is not affiliated with, authorized, or endorsed by any console maker, software developer, or publisher. All avatar format protocols and grid specs are properties of their respective owners.

About.title: About LifeSimGrid
About.intro: We're an independent open-source team building front-end creative tools and avatar data configurators for life sim fans worldwide.
About.coreMissionText: Our goal: a one-stop, privacy-first toolkit for life sim fans and retro 8-bit enthusiasts. All three studios are built on open, universal web tech — no downloads, no sign-ups, no servers:
About.feature1: Uses the HTML5 Canvas API for high-precision multi-ratio grid sampling (Square, Book Cover, Interior Wide) with four density levels. The built-in 32-color retro palette and paint-by-numbers system solve the color and edge distortion pain points hobbyists face when hand-tracing templates.
About.feature2: Based on the public binary buffer protocol, helps users optimize, reconstruct, and unlock their own avatar QR code data. Supports single-file editing and batch ZIP export — 20+ files in under a second.
About.feature3: Uses the Web Audio API's low-level synthesis to simulate retro 8-bit electronic voice frequency curves. Includes 5 voice presets, custom phrase TTS, a 16-type personality matrix, and a relationship leaderboard with zodiac + personality compatibility scoring.
About.openSourceText: We believe in open source and pure front-end tech. All code is on GitHub under the MIT License. No backend servers — just the cleanest, most efficient fan toolkit on the web. Contributions, translations, and bug reports are always welcome.
About.techStackText: Built with Next.js 16 (App Router), TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and next-intl for 10-language i18n. Static-exported and deployed on Cloudflare Pages with global CDN. Core libraries: jsQR (QR decoding), qrcode (QR encoding), JSZip (batch export), and Lucide Icons.
About.privacyFirstText: Every feature runs 100% client-side. No backend, no database, no analytics, no tracking. Your images, avatar data, and voice settings never leave your browser. You can verify this yourself by opening DevTools → Network tab — zero outbound data requests.
About.communityText: LifeSimGrid is a community-driven project. Whether you're a developer, translator, or just a passionate fan — we'd love your help. Submit Issues, open Pull Requests, or help improve translations in our 10 supported languages. Together, we build the best fan toolkit on the web.

Privacy.title: Privacy Policy
Privacy.intro: This policy explains how this site ('we') handles your data. User privacy is our absolute top priority.
Privacy.corePrinciple: 1. Core Data Principle (100% Client-Side Local Processing)
Privacy.corePrincipleText: This site is serverless. When you upload images, photos, or avatar QR codes for pixelation or decoding, everything — Canvas sampling, Buffer byte rewriting, audio synthesis — runs right in your browser in real time. We don't have a backend database, and we can't transmit, dump, or store any of your files.
Privacy.localStorageText: The Voice Lab's Relationship Leaderboard saves your resident roster (up to 15 residents with names, zodiac signs, and personality types) in your browser's localStorage under the key 'lifesimgrid-residents'. Additionally, each studio's History feature stores your recent action records (up to 20 entries per studio, including thumbnails and settings) in your browser's IndexedDB under the database 'lifesimgrid-history'. Both localStorage and IndexedDB data never leave your device — they are not sent to any server. You can clear history at any time by clicking 'Clear All' in each studio's History panel, or by clearing your browser data. No cookies are used for these features.
Privacy.cookiesText: To cover hosting and CDN costs, this site may display non-tracking, high-quality tech/design ads via networks like Google AdSense, Carbon Ads, or EthicalAds. These advertisers may use Cookies and Web Beacons to collect non-personally identifiable info (browser type, OS, visit time, etc.) for ad relevance. You can refuse or clear Cookies in your browser settings at any time — this won't affect any core tool features.
Privacy.thirdPartyText:
This site uses the following open-source JavaScript libraries, all running client-side: jsQR (MIT License, QR code decoding), qrcode (MIT License, QR code generation), JSZip (MIT License, ZIP file creation), and Lucide (ISC License, icons). None of these libraries transmit data externally.

Additionally, this site may use Google Analytics 4 (GA4) and Microsoft Clarity to collect anonymized usage data for understanding visitor behavior and improving site performance. These services may use cookies or similar tracking technologies. You can opt out of Google Analytics by using the Google Analytics opt-out browser add-on, and you can disable Clarity by adjusting your browser settings. These analytics services are optional and can be disabled without affecting core tool functionality.
Privacy.childrenText: This site is a general-audience tool and does not knowingly collect personal information from anyone, including children under 13. Since no data is collected or transmitted, COPPA and similar regulations are inherently satisfied. If you're a parent and have concerns, rest assured that your child's activity on this site generates zero data footprint.
Privacy.gdprText: Because this site processes all data client-side and does not collect, store, or transmit any personal data, it inherently complies with GDPR (EU) and CCPA (California). There is no personal data to access, export, or delete because we never have it. If you clear your browser's localStorage, all locally stored data (resident roster) is permanently removed.
Privacy.securityText: Since your data never leaves your device, there's zero risk of server-side breaches. We're committed to keeping our code transparent — review the full data flow anytime in our GitHub repo. The site is served over HTTPS to prevent man-in-the-middle attacks during page delivery.

Terms.title: Terms of Service
Terms.intro: By using this site, you agree to these terms. Please read them carefully.
Terms.natureText: The tools on this site (Pixel Studio, Data Configurator, Audio Lab) are technical aids built on public web standards. We don't provide cracking software, contact official game servers, or distribute copyrighted game assets. All processing happens in your browser — nothing is transmitted externally. Each studio automatically saves your recent action history (up to 20 entries per studio) in your browser's IndexedDB for easy review — this data never leaves your device.
Terms.acceptableText: You agree to use this site only for lawful purposes. You must not: (a) use the tools to create or distribute content that infringes on others' intellectual property; (b) attempt to reverse-engineer, decompile, or disrupt the site's code or infrastructure; (c) use automated scripts to scrape or overload the site; (d) misrepresent the source or ownership of any generated data. Violations may result in IP-level access restrictions.
Terms.liabilityText: When using these tools to generate pixel templates or modify avatar data, you must comply with your local laws and not infringe on third-party rights. This site is a technical platform only. We will not be liable for any direct, indirect, incidental, or punitive damages from your use of generated data on third-party platforms — including device failures, account issues, or legal disputes.
Terms.ipText: All original front-end interfaces, UI, and core code are under the MIT License. This is an independent third-party project. Generic technical terms (avatar protocol formats, 85×85 grid specs) refer to IP owned by their respective holders. We don't represent any console maker or game publisher.
Terms.availabilityText: This site is provided 'as is' and 'as available.' We don't guarantee uninterrupted access, error-free operation, or compatibility with all browsers and devices. We may modify, suspend, or discontinue any feature at any time without prior notice. Static deployment on Cloudflare Pages provides high availability, but we make no uptime warranties.
Terms.terminationText: You may stop using this site at any time — simply close the browser tab. Since we don't collect personal data or require accounts, there's nothing to cancel or delete. Locally stored data (resident roster and history records) can be cleared via the 'Clear All' button or your browser settings.
Terms.governingText: These terms shall be governed by and construed in accordance with applicable laws, without regard to conflict-of-law principles. Any disputes arising from the use of this site shall be resolved through good-faith negotiation before any formal proceedings.
Terms.changesText: We may update these terms at any time. Changes take effect immediately upon publication. Continued use of the site after changes constitutes acceptance of the revised terms. We encourage you to review this page periodically.

Contact.title: Contact Us
Contact.intro: We love hearing from the community — bug reports, algorithm suggestions, or translation corrections are all welcome. Choose the channel that fits your needs below.
Contact.githubText: Visit our GitHub repo to submit Issues or Pull Requests. Bug reports, feature requests, and translation improvements are all welcome. Global developers and contributors of all skill levels are encouraged to participate!
Contact.supportText: Having trouble parsing specific avatar data? QR codes not scanning correctly? Voice synthesis not playing? Send the details (including screenshots or QR code images if possible) to support@lifesimgrid.com. An actual engineer will look into it within 48 hours.
Contact.legalText: We respect all intellectual property and legal regulations. For compliance questions about the generic tech protocols used on this site, or if you believe any content infringes on your rights, reach out to legal@lifesimgrid.com. We take all legitimate concerns seriously and will respond promptly.
Contact.faqText: Many common questions are already answered in our FAQ section on the homepage. Check there first — you might find your answer instantly! Topics include privacy, QR parsing errors, voice synthesis details, and compatibility calculations.
Contact.responseNoteText: We're a small volunteer team. Bug reports and security issues are prioritized and typically addressed within 48 hours. Feature requests and translation corrections may take longer. Thank you for your patience and for being part of the community!

Footer.copyright: © {year} LifeSimGrid. Made with ❤️ by fans, for fans. Non-profit, educational fan tool.
Footer.techDisclaimer: An independent third-party client-side toolkit. Not affiliated with or endorsed by any console maker or game developer.
